---
description: Parametric orchestrator — `/invoke <acts...> [-- task]` runs one specialist per act (intel once, cumulative artifacts, closers once). Acts audit spec plan test impl refactor debug design clean review docs verify security.
---
<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by ~/.claude/hooks/gen-invoke-commands.py (parametric v3) — do not edit by hand; edit the config/model-policy and re-run the generator. -->

# /invoke &lt;acts...&gt; [-- task]

Run any combination of acts in ONE flow, e.g. `/invoke audit plan impl -- add rate limiting`.
Acts always run in this canonical order regardless of how they are typed:

| act | specialist agent | artifact | model | role |
|-----|------------------|----------|-------|------|
| `audit` | audit-specialist | `AUDIT-{date}.md` | sonnet | tech-debt / forensic / architecture audit |
| `spec` | spec-architect | `SPEC-{slug}.md` | sonnet | build-ready spec + contracts |
| `plan` | planning-director | `plan-{date}-{slug}.md` | sonnet | phased execution plan |
| `test` | test-author | `TEST-REPORT.md` | sonnet | author failing tests first (TDD red) |
| `impl` | implementation-engineer | `IMPL-REPORT.md` | opus | implement the plan (RUNS ON OPUS) |
| `refactor` | refactor-specialist | `REFACTOR-REPORT.md` | sonnet | behavior-preserving refactor (test-guarded) |
| `debug` | debug-detective | `ROOTCAUSE.md` | sonnet | root-cause an unknown failure |
| `design` | frontend-uiux-designer | `DESIGN-REPORT.md` | opus | UI/UX design + polish (opus) |
| `clean` | deadcode-reaper | `REAP-REPORT.md` | sonnet | dead-code reap (code-mutating) |
| `review` | santa-reviewer | `SANTA-REVIEW.md` | opus | Santa Method adversarial review (opus) |
| `docs` | docs-sync-agent | `DOCS-SYNC-REPORT.md` | sonnet | sync docs to the change |
| `verify` | qa-verifier | `VERIFY-REPORT.md` | sonnet | verify behavior end-to-end |
| `security` | security-sentinel | `SECURITY-REPORT.md` | sonnet | security review / hardening |

An unknown token is reported ("valid acts: …") and the flow proceeds with the recognized acts.

## ACT A — INTEL (once, yourself, no dispatch)

1. jcodemunch `plan_turn` (query = task) + `assemble_task_context` on the impacted area.
2. graphify `graph_stats` + `query_graph` (freshness — rebuild only if a guard said STALE).
3. Read `CODEX.md` + the repo root→target `CLAUDE.md` (dox tree).
4. Produce the **BRIEF** (task, surfaces FE/BE/infra, impacted layers, contracts at risk, paths).

Every specialist below receives this BRIEF **plus the cumulative artifact set** from acts already
completed — later acts start warm and never re-derive earlier context.

## ACT B — DISPATCH (one specialist per requested act, canonical order)

For each requested act, spawn its agent (Agent tool, `subagent_type` = the agent above) with the
description prefix `[<model>] ` from the table and `model:"<model>"`, passing the BRIEF + **every
prior artifact**. Wait for the act's artifact before the next act.

- **`impl` RUNS ON OPUS (mandatory)** — `implementation-engineer` gets `[opus]` + `model:"opus"`
  (model-policy `invoke_categories.IMPLEMENT`). When PLAN already ran, its plan artifact is the
  implementation input and the `requires: plan` note is satisfied — do not re-ask.
- **`design`** dispatches `frontend-uiux-designer` (opus); assets come from Higgsfield
  (`higgsfield-generate`) per the frontend asset mandate.
- **`clean`** is code-mutating — treat REAP edits as real changes (audit + verify after).

## ACT C — CLOSERS (once, after all acts)

Run the closers chain on the cumulative result: deadcode-reaper, santa-reviewer, docs-sync-agent, qa-verifier.

## FALLBACK — legacy inline rosters (dispatch unavailable)

If the Agent tool is unavailable, invoke each act's skills yourself via the Skill tool, in order,
executing every act sequentially. Never silently drop an act.
